Name: Brennan2019: EEG during Naturalistic Listening to Alice in Wonderland
Acknowledgements: EEG data were collected between March 2015 and December 2016 at the Computational Neurolinguistics Lab (CNL Lab), Department of Linguistics, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. Per-word linguistic annotations (lexical frequency, sound power, word length, and hierarchical n-gram / RNN / CFG surprisal predictors) and per-subject preprocessing outputs are re-hosted from the Deep Blue Data record (v2, DOI 10.7302/746w-g237) and the cnllab/alice-eeg-shared repository.
HowToAcknowledge: Please cite: Brennan, J. R., & Hale, J. T. (2019). Hierarchical structure guides rapid linguistic predictions during naturalistic listening. PLoS ONE, 14(1), e0207741. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0207741 ; and the source data record: Brennan, J. R. (2023). EEG Datasets for Naturalistic Listening to 'Alice in Wonderland' (v2). University of Michigan - Deep Blue Data. https://doi.org/10.7302/746w-g237
EthicsApprovals: University of Michigan Institutional Review Board (Health Sciences and Behavioral Sciences)
